MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES Via CourtCall:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Carol Chulick, AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO AND AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR OF JOHN CHULICK DECEASED; Deborah Hagen and Joleen Hagler, AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR OF JOHN CHULICK DECEASED.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Columbia Mechanical Contractors, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted as to Defendant's Exhibits A, C, D, and E (Plaintiffs' Complaint, 1st Amended Complaint, 2nd Amended Complaint, and 3rd Amended Complaint). (Evidence Code section 452(d).) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent John Chulick was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Robert Cantley's testimony that he could only recall one job site (the Russ Building) that he worked at with decedent is insufficient to show that it was the only job site that he worked at with decedent. (Defendant's Index of Exhibits, Ex. Q at 479:1-4.) There is no evidence that Mr. Cantley was asked about Defendant's work around decedent other than at the Russ Building. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal. App. 4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Additionally, there is no evidence that Mr. Cantley was asked about or agreed to the job being in March 1971. (Ex. Q at 596:14-22.) Regardless, Mr. Cantley's testimony versus the declaration of Valerie Howerton Miller stating that Defendant did not perform work prior to August 1971 is a triable issue of fact. (Defendant's Index of Exhibits, Ex. R.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Sep-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
T. Eric Sun of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and opposing party Columbia Mechanical Contractors, Inc.
Solomon Pantuch of HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG (415-766-3200) appearing via CourtCall for defendants and opposing party Frank Bonetti Plumbing, Inc. and Marconi Plastering Company, Inc.
Ian P. Dillon of WFBM, LLP (714-634-2522), appearing via CourtCall for defendants FDCC California, Inc., opposing parties George H. Wilson Company, Inc. and D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.
Todd Thacker of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and opposing party Cosco Fire Protection, Inc.
James N. Sinunu of SINUNU BRUNI, LLP (415-362-9700), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and conditional non-opposing party Rosendin Electric, Inc.
Richard Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing via CourtCall for Intervenor, Fireman's Fund Insurance Company & American Employers Insurance Company.
Julie Hayashida of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing via CourtCall for defendant W.L. Hickey Sons, Inc.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Advancing the Trial Date with Preference; and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Oppositions filed by D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc. and George H. Wilson, Inc. with joinder and conditional non-opposition by Columbia Mechanical Contractors, Inc. Conditional non-oppositions by Scott Co. of California et al., Cooper Brothers, Inc., and Rosendin Electric, Inc. Conditional opposition by W. L. Hickey Sons Inc. The Court did not consider the opposition by Monterey Mechanical Co., and thereby Cosco Fire Protection Inc.'s joinder, or Frank Bonetti Plumbing, Inc. for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.)

The trial date of March 28, 2022 is vacated. The new trial date is December 27, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is January 7, 2022. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 23, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 10, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 24, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 8, 2021. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
